texto: What we can do now to bring about his kingdom of justice is to cooperate in the establishment of justice in that portion of his kingdom over which we have some immediate control: ourselves. Let us begin the pursuit of justice and peace by cleaning up our own lives and establishing peace within our own hearts.
explicacao: Citação de M. Basil Pennington sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “What we can do now to bring about his kingdom of justice is to cooperate in the establishment of …” articula amor, christian-spirituality, paz em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como paz.

Let us begin the pursuit of justice and peace by cleaning up our own lives and establishing peace within our own hearts.

texto: We ought not to READ the scriptures, but listen to them, for our Beloved is present and speaks to us through them. (vs. it being like a love letter sent to us from afar).
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M. Basil Pennington: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “We ought not to READ the scriptures, but listen to them, for our Beloved is present and speaks to…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, bible, ought pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: We ought not to READ the scriptures, but listen to them, for our Beloved is present and speaks to us through them. (vs. it being like a love letter sent to us from afar).

texto: In the silence, whether we listen to the creation around us, the words of revelation, or the deepest stirrings of our own hearts, we begin to perceive another voice, one that is too often lost in the static of life. It is no use saying: ”Speak, Lord, your servant wants to hear,” if We never risk the silence to listen. 48
explicacao: Citação de M. Basil Pennington sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “In the silence, whether we listen to the creation around us, the words of revelation, or the deep…” articula amor, christian-spirituality, meditation em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e meditation.

frase_original: In the silence, whether we listen to the creation around us, the words of revelation, or the deepest stirrings of our own hearts, we begin to perceive another voice, one that is too often lost in the static of life. It is no use saying: ”Speak, Lord, your servant wants to hear,” if We never risk the silence to listen. 48
